step_by_step_thinking: Based on the medical literature reviewed, obstructive sleep apnoea is closely linked to facial skeletal anomalies, specifically involving retrusion of the mandible or maxilla. Given the context provided about mandibular issues leading to potential airway compromise, bilateral TMJ ankylosis would severely restrict mandibular motion and therefore exacerbate any existing airway obstruction issues characteristic of obstructive sleep apnoea.
raw_response:
Based on the medical literature reviewed, obstructive sleep apnoea is closely linked to facial skeletal anomalies, specifically involving retrusion of the mandible or maxilla. Given the context provided about mandibular issues leading to potential airway compromise, bilateral TMJ ankylosis would severely restrict mandibular motion and therefore exacerbate any existing airway obstruction issues characteristic of obstructive sleep apnoea.
\boxed{D}
